CVE-2018-18926.title: Gitea before 1.5.4 allows remote code execution because it does n ...
CVE-2018-18926.summary: Gitea before 1.5.4 allows remote code execution because it does not properly validate session IDs. This is related to session ID handling in the go-macaron/session code for Macaron.

CVE-2019-1010261.title: Gitea 1.7.0 and earlier is affected by: Cross Site Scripting (XSS ...
CVE-2019-1010261.summary: Gitea 1.7.0 and earlier is affected by: Cross Site Scripting (XSS). The impact is: Attacker is able to have victim execute arbitrary JS in browser. The component is: go-get URL generation - PR to fix: https://github.com/go-gitea/gitea/pull/5905. The attack vector is: victim must open a specifically crafted URL. The fixed version is: 1.7.1 and later.

CVE-2019-1010314.title: Gitea 1.7.2, 1.7.3 is affected by: Cross Site Scripting (XSS). Th ...
CVE-2019-1010314.summary: Gitea 1.7.2, 1.7.3 is affected by: Cross Site Scripting (XSS). The impact is: execute JavaScript in victim's browser, when the vulnerable repo page is loaded. The component is: repository's description. The attack vector is: victim must navigate to public and affected repo page.

CVE-2019-11576.title: Gitea before 1.8.0 allows 1FA for user accounts that have complet ...
CVE-2019-11576.summary: Gitea before 1.8.0 allows 1FA for user accounts that have completed 2FA enrollment. If a user's credentials are known, then an attacker could send them to the API without requiring the 2FA one-time password.

CVE-2021-45325.title: Server Side Request Forgery (SSRF) vulneraility exists in Gitea b ...
CVE-2021-45325.summary: Server Side Request Forgery (SSRF) vulneraility exists in Gitea before 1.7.0 using the OpenID URL.

CVE-2021-45326.title: Cross Site Request Forgery (CSRF) vulnerability exists in Gitea b ...
CVE-2021-45326.summary: Cross Site Request Forgery (CSRF) vulnerability exists in Gitea before 1.5.2 via API routes.This can be dangerous especially with state altering POST requests.

CVE-2021-45327.title: Gitea before 1.11.2 is affected by Trusting HTTP Permission Metho ...
CVE-2021-45327.summary: Gitea before 1.11.2 is affected by Trusting HTTP Permission Methods on the Server Side when referencing the vulnerable admin or user API. which could let a remote malisious user execute arbitrary code.

CVE-2021-45328.title: Gitea before 1.4.3 is affected by URL Redirection to Untrusted Si ...
CVE-2021-45328.summary: Gitea before 1.4.3 is affected by URL Redirection to Untrusted Site ('Open Redirect') via internal URLs.

CVE-2021-45329.title: Cross Site Scripting (XSS) vulnerability exists in Gitea before 1 ...
CVE-2021-45329.summary: Cross Site Scripting (XSS) vulnerability exists in Gitea before 1.5.1 via the repository settings inside the external wiki/issue tracker URL field.

CVE-2022-38183.title: In Gitea before 1.16.9, it was possible for users to add existing ...
CVE-2022-38183.summary: In Gitea before 1.16.9, it was possible for users to add existing issues to projects. Due to improper access controls, an attacker could assign any issue to any project in Gitea (there was no permission check for fetching the issue). As a result, the attacker would get access to private issue titles.
